museum.name: Gaming Time Machine
museum.dek: A summer month of previews and pressure: Star Fox 64 lands in North America, GoldenEye approaches, and the UK N64 shelf starts to look livelier.
museum.curatorNote: July is treated as a bridge month. Star Fox 64's North American release and post-E3 coverage are the safest anchors; UK notes stay contextual.

sections.title: News
sections.subtitle: The year begins turning toward autumn.

items.title: Star Fox 64 carries rumble into North America
items.body: Released at the turn of July in North America, Star Fox 64 made the Rumble Pak a public-facing N64 feature outside Japan.
items.sourceNote: Sources list Star Fox 64 around June 30/July 1, 1997 in North America; kept as turn-of-July context.

items.title: GoldenEye waits in the wings
items.body: Rare's Bond game was approaching release, but expectations were not yet fixed. It still looked like a licensed shooter arriving late for a 1995 film.
items.sourceNote: GoldenEye released in Japan and North America in August 1997, with UK/Europe date references varying between August and November depending on source.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII becomes a Western marketing object
items.body: The Japanese phenomenon was now being translated into Western anticipation: trailers, magazine spreads, and the promise of a September North American launch.
items.sourceNote: Final Fantasy VII Western releases followed in September and November 1997.

sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: A summer shelf of arrivals abroad and near-arrivals at home.

items.title: Star Fox 64
items.body: North American release window for Nintendo's rail shooter and Rumble Pak showcase.
items.sourceNote: Sources differ by June 30/July 1; represented conservatively.

items.title: Mario Kart 64
items.body: Fresh on European shelves from late June, now becoming part of summer multiplayer routines.

items.title: Final Fantasy VII
items.body: Still not out in the West, but already occupying the kind of space usually reserved for released games.
items.sourceNote: Japan January; North America September; Europe November.

items.title: Force feedback becomes imaginable
items.body: Even before Lylat Wars arrived in Europe, rumble coverage made the N64 controller feel unfinished without an accessory in the back.
items.sourceNote: Star Fox 64/Rumble Pak context from 1997 releases.

items.title: PC graphics cards become aspirational
items.body: The PC future looked smoother for players with the right 3D accelerator, and slightly punishing for everyone else.

sections.title: Magazine Covers
sections.subtitle: Post-E3 issues turned rumour into a queue.

items.title: GoldenEye previews
items.headline: Bond before the phenomenon
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed N64 magazine-style cover image standing in for July 1997.
items.curatorNote: Before GoldenEye became a living-room legend, it was a preview item asking to be believed.
items.body: Before GoldenEye became a living-room legend, it was a preview item asking to be believed.

items.title: RPG import coverage
items.headline: Final Fantasy VII approaches the West
items.coverImageAlt: Reconstructed PlayStation magazine-style cover image standing in for July 1997.
items.curatorNote: The magazine page taught readers how large a PlayStation RPG could look.
items.body: The magazine page taught readers how large a PlayStation RPG could look.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: Summer rumours moved faster online, but the printed version still had weight.

items.title: GoldenEye chatter was pre-social media
items.body: Expectation moved through forums, fan pages, and playground confidence rather than feeds.

items.title: PC hardware talk was already networked
items.body: Drivers, patches, and card recommendations circulated online for players following the shooter future.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: July felt like looking through the window of autumn.

items.title: Everything important seemed nearly here
items.body: GoldenEye, Final Fantasy VII, and the winter PC shooters all felt close enough to start arguing about.

items.title: Accessories felt like secrets
items.body: Rumble sounded like a gimmick until the idea of feeling a hit started to stick.

sources.label: Star Fox 64 release context
sources.note: Used for 1997 Star Fox 64/Rumble Pak context; date represented cautiously.
